100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 9, 1926, the British Empire faced a potential crisis as a general strike loomed in response to wage disputes affecting coal miners. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate debated the future of Prohibition, with increasing calls for reform as public sentiment shifted. In Europe, Germany continued to struggle with economic instability, exacerbated by war reparations. These developments highlight a period of significant social and political tension across the globe.
